entry.title: Change SAC Story Data source from HANA Model to SAP Datasphere Model
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have requirement to change SAC Story Data source from HANA Model to SAP Datasphere Model as we are migrating from SAP HANA Cloud to SAP Datasphere. We have hundreds of SAC stories built on hundreds of Live SAP HANA Cloud CALC views direct connection SAC models. As we are migrating From HANA Cloud to SAP Datasphere what is a best approach for a minimal impact/efforts on existing SAC Stories.

entry.content:
Hi,

Is it mandatory that we must activate below business functions when migrating from EhP8 6.0 to S/4 HANA 2023 in SAP Enterprise Asset Management area?

At this moment not all Business Functions mentioned are active in ECC and we plan to move to S/4 HANA 2023.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4cef93946a0b48ec89533b3c34443b85/1655de4728664d1e9c99e9d3e67770a7.html

LOG_EAM_CI_1 to  LOG_EAM_CI_12
LOG_EAM_SIMPLICITY to LOG_EAM_SIMPLICITY_12
LOG_EAM_IME_1

Regards

Vineeth

entry.title: Re: 1/3 From JavaScript to TypeScript: How We Enhanced Code Reliability in SAPUI5 Development
entry.content:
Nice introduction to your blog post series. When you mentioned TS acting as mentor that helps with guidance, I immediately thought: are you also using linting like eslint (which is more likely) or (my preferred alternative nowadays) biome?

When it comes to biome especially, I thought that its CLI is really neat in helping one to improve or "question" the JS code you write. As always it could also lead to over-optimization when not (yet) necessary (in case of forEach usage) but each rule can be configured. In other cases like with CSS units for example it can be really helpful. I really like the embedded direct link to MDN for most of the rules in biome, trying to not only show squiggly red lines but help you why and improve it at the same time. Of course, reading the rules themselves for whatever linter you're using, also helps.

TS in combination with those tools (if you pass configuration hell, depending on project size/setup :P) is truly a new world. Even if it may be "limiting" to some, at times. 😉

entry.title: Canada ECP- Cross-year retro payroll and tax for Canada Payroll
entry.content:
Hello,

This is a question for Canada payroll (SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll).

We have a group of salaried employees paid bi-weekly. These employees are in Ontario and Quebec province. Payroll is processed before the end of pay period and employees are paid in advance. Time related information from external system is on a two weeks lag. 

For example: When payroll is processed for first pay period (PP) of 2025, employees are paid the bi-weekly salary for PP01 and any paid/unpaid absence for PP26/2024 will be processed with PP01/2025. When payroll is processed for PP02/2025, employees are paid the bi-weekly salary for PP02 and any paid/unpaid absence for PP01 will be processed with PP02.

During the year, we do not have issues with this scenario because earnings and taxes are adjusted in the period the data is applicable. However, the issue is specific to the scenario when absences are entered in current year for prior year and the absence has reduced the regular earnings. 

By default, standard SAP system does not change/recalculate taxes during cross-year retro. This is configured per payroll function KACYT and parameter ADJU. This is working as expected and we do not want to change this process. We want to bring forward the difference in earnings from prior year into the current year and calculate tax accordingly on the reduced earning. To achieve this, we configured the outflow/inflow processing, but inflow of regular earnings into current year is creating /103 and /153 (for Quebec), and the system does not calculate any tax on the negative inflow. Therefore, the tax calculated and displayed on pay statement is high as compared to the total earnings on the pay statement because the tax is calculated on the earnings in current pay period and no tax is calculated on the negative non-periodic income. Employee is not refunded for the taxes on reduced earnings for prior year.

 

For example: 

Regular earnings for PP01/2025 = $4,000

Difference in earnings from PP26/2024 due to unpaid absence for PP26/2024 after original period was processed = -$2,400

Total earnings displayed on pay statement for PP01/2025 = $1,600

/102 in PP01/2025 = $4,000

/103 in PP01/2025 = -$2,400 (negative inflow from PP26/2024)

/152 in PP01/2025 = $4,000

/153 in PP01/2025 = -$2,400 (negative inflow from PP26/2024)

Tax is calculated only on /102 and /152 in PP01/2025

Anyone having a similar scenario to get negative inflow from prior year into current year and implemented any solution to ensure the correct taxes are calculated according to the earnings displayed on the pay statement?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: One-Time Payment Not Generating for Time Account Payout
entry.content:
Cindy-

One thing that springs to mind is that pay components are individually permissioned in SF. You might be missing this permission.

entry.title: Reforma Tributária LC 214 - Nota Fiscal Writer J1B1N/J1B2N
entry.content:
Oi pessoal,

Após aplicar as Notas SAP para as novas funcionalidades para atender a reforma tributária da LC 214.

3561376 - Brazilian Tax Reform: Delivery and Support Guidelines

3552901 - Tax Reform: Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3552903

3552903 - Tax Reform: Tax Groups and Tax Types Added to Nota Fiscal

Na J1B1N/J1B2N está ocorrendo erro devido o programa não considerar os novos Tax Groups CBS, IBSS, IBSM.

Conforme abaixo, está consitindo somente ICMS, ICST, ISS, IPI, ISSS, ISSP sem considerar os novos Tax Groups.

No meu teste estou usando o CBS1.

Devido a isso, ocorre o erro abaixo.

Já conseguiram resolver ou sabem se existe SAP Note para corrigir isso?

Obrigado.

entry.title: Late comer detection with rounding enabled
entry.content: Hi we are using Clock in Clock out integration with SuccessFactors Time.  Our requirement is to have the time rounded to the closest 15 minutes for valuation purposes. We are following the "standard" rounding functionality which looks up the closest time when the time is saved in external time data. This is working great, except for one thing. We have another requirement that late comer detection should flag someone as late if they arrive 3 minutes late. Since the rounding is set to 7.5 minutes or less rounds to the top of the hour (when the shift begins) this means employees can only be considered late if they are 7.5 minutes late rather than the 3 minutes that is the requirement. Is there any possible workaround for this?

entry.title: Re: 3/3 From JavaScript to TypeScript: The Shocking Results of Our SAPUI5 Transformation
entry.content:
1. Centralized Declarations: All type definitions, enums, and interfaces are stored in a single declarations file to maintain consistency and ensure changes propagate across the codebase.

I wouldn't say that this should be a binary decision. Instead of repeating, I'd like to simply reference this great blog post from Matt Pocock. In the end I agree with him and also prefer a mix of both: colocation and shared in a single file/location, based on their usage (classic: "it depends"). Also strictly putting it _all_ into a single file makes the file change often, doesn't it? But if it works for you, that's great!

 

Might be more niche of a question but: How do you handle the selection of your UI5 types or are you fortunate enough to be working with the latest and greatest?

What I mean with that is the mismatch in older type versions where there is a tradeoff in choosing one over the other. Example: UI5 version X is in use and you're using some "Event" that is only typed in a newer version of the types, which relates or corresponds to UI5 version Y. Now you'd have tradeoffs also when it comes to the part "searching in the SDK/Documentation" as using the newer types may confuse the team with properties/methods etc. that aren't in your current UI5 version yet but at least you can type your Event.

 

One more, rather broad & meta question would be: How is your team keeping up with news in TS-land? Is every developer encouraged to bring up new additions, changes, fixes of TS into the team. Or is it more of a top down approach where the more senior dev in the team uses some of their time to keep tabs on news, tries out things (also wrt to the "improvements of the Architecture") and brings those back into the team or the projects their working on? Both? Happy to hear more about this rather technical side of the team structure when it comes to tooling/tech used in the projects!

 

Great closing blog post to the series! 🙂

entry.title: Re: JSON to XML Conversion Namespace in SAP CPI
entry.content: The error is tied to the message mapping so I would recommend tracing the flow and testing the payload in simulate mode for the message mapping. The error indicates that a mandatory element in the target type is not generated with the payload used as the mapping input.

Hi SAP Melbourne Community,

Come and join us for the 1st SAP Inside Track for this year 2025.

Date: 19th feb 2025

Time : 6pm to 8.30 pm

Location: Level 13, 555 Collins Street, Melbourne (Amazon Building)

Note : Please bring your ID card

Agenda :

6.00pm – Catchup with Community ( Drinks will be served)

6.30pm– Topic 1

7pm – Topic 2

7.30 pm – Catchup with SAP Community (Drinks and Pizza will be served)

8.30 pm – Wrap up

 

Our friends at AWS have kindly agreed to host us again.
Would like to thank AWS for their continued support. 

Thankyou!

See you there

Cheers,

SAP Community

entry.content:

I do have a lot of flat files to be uploaded in a LOCAL TABLE. The problem is some of the fields were defined as DATE but the date format in the CSV files were in the format MM/DD/YYYY.

How do you fix this error aside from manually editing the DATE fields based on the suggested format such YYYY-MM-DD?

Thanks.

Loed

entry.title: Dependent Dropdowns in ALV Grid (OOPs ALV)
entry.content:
I am new to ABAP and currently working on an ALV Grid using Object-Oriented ALV (OOPs ALV) in SAP ABAP. I have two columns in my ALV, both of which are dropdowns:

  1. Data Type: This column has dropdown options - "Number" and "Text".
  2. Conversion: This column's dropdown options should depend on the selection in the "Data Type" column.

Here's the behavior I need:

  • If "Number" is selected in the "Data Type" column, the "Conversion" column should show dropdown options like "Sum" and "Division".
  • If "Text" is selected in the "Data Type" column, the "Conversion" column should show dropdown options like "Upper" and "Lower".

How can I implement this dynamic dropdown behavior in an ALV Grid using the OOPs approach? Any guidance would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Change SAC Story Data source from HANA Model to SAP D...

Cross live model replace data source is NOT supported. Please refer to below document for details.  For your case, stories should more or less rebuid with DSP model. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/8ccad51728324559aac312c2e1d72ea5.html

entry.title: Re: Compensation plan template is not visible in Import E...
entry.content:
I faced the same issue, and it was resolved when I disabled some old templates from Managing Form Templates.
It seems there is a limitation in displaying templates when using Basic Import → More Options... → Compensation Options → Compensation Template:

A specific number of templates can be displayed.

Mohamad Alshehabi

entry.title: Re: Dependents data is not appearing under "Workflow Deta...
entry.content:
Hello,

The possible reason for this behavior could be RBP. If in the workflow you have set the field 'Respect Permission' to 'Yes' then the field will not appear in workflow details if he/she(approver) has no permission for the given fields.

Additionally check the Target Employee Groups for the given Manager.

However, if the field is appearing in workflow details with blank data then please refer this 2901124 - Unable to See Details of a Workflow Regardless of Permissions in Employee Central

 

Thankyou.

entry.title: Re: One-Time Payment Not Generating for Time Account Payout
entry.content:
Hello,

I can see in the Pay Component configuration that you have attached here,

The field 'Display on Self Service' is set 'No'. So when this field is set to 'No' then the Pay Component will not appear in drop down even if you have enabled RBP.

So set this field to 'Yes' and try again, it will work. 

 

Thankyou.

entry.title: Preprinted Print format in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

We are experiencing an alignment issue when printing preprinted A4 forms from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using Adobe Forms. We have designed the form in Adobe LiveCycle Designer and have configured it in SAP, but when printing, the alignment does not match the preprinted template.

Here are the details of the issue:

  • We are using Adobe Forms to design preprinted A4 formats.
  • The alignment of fields and text is not correct when we print the form from SAP to the physical printer.
  • I have attached the printer model and the print output, but the issue persists.

Could anyone kindly assist in resolving this alignment issue?

Troubleshooting Steps Taken So Far:

  1. Printer Settings & Configuration: We’ve ensured that the printer is set to A4 paper size and checked the margins, but alignment still seems off.
  2. Adobe Form Layout: The Adobe Form layout has been double-checked in Adobe LiveCycle Designer, and the fields are aligned correctly.
  3. SAP Output Configuration: We’ve reviewed the output device settings and ensured that paper size and printer driver configurations are correct.
  4. Scaling Settings: We confirmed that scaling options like "Fit to Page" are not selected during printing.
  5. Print Preview: We checked the print preview in SAP, and the layout appears correctly there, but the printed output is misaligned.

We would appreciate any guidance on what could be causing the misalignment, especially if there are any SAP or printer-specific configurations that need to be adjusted.

Thank you for your help in advance!

Kind regards,

Prince

entry.title: Product Change Logs in EWM Warehouse Monitor
entry.content:
SAP Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA offers a feature to track and display product change logs directly within the Warehouse Monitor. This functionality enhances transparency by enabling users to monitor modifications made to warehouse products. Below is a step-by-step guide to activating and utilizing this feature.

Activating Change Document Recording:

By default, change document recording for warehouse products is not activated in the system. To enable this functionality, follow these steps:

We can activate the change document recording for the Warehouse product by following the below SPRO customizing path.

SPRO ---> SCM Extended Warehouse Management ----> Extended Warehouse Management ---->Master Data ----> Product -----> Activate Change Documents.On the activation screen, select the entry for "Product (Warehouse-Specific)" and click on "New Entries."Check both options to enable logging for changes in the "Warehouse Data" view and the "Storage Type Data" view.
Save your changes. These settings can also be transported to other systems.

Testing the Functionality:

To verify that the change document recording is active: Open transaction /SCWM/MAT1 (Maintain Warehouse Product).
Select a product, e.g., TG-100.
Make a change, such as modifying the process type determination indicator (e.g., changing from TG to RM).
Save the changes.

Viewing Product Change Logs:

Once the change document recording is active, you can view the logs using two methods:

1. Using Warehouse Monitor:

SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Master Data → Product → Display Change Documents.

Open transaction /SCWM/MON.
Select the node for "Product Master Data."
Enter the product details and execute.
Navigate to "More Methods" and choose "Display Change Documents." This will show all changes made at the warehouse product level2.Using Transaction Code /SCWM/PROD_CD_READ

Execute /SCWM/PROD_CD_READ.
Enter the following details:
User: Specify the user who made changes.
Date & Time: Define the range for tracking changes.
Product: Input the product code.
Warehouse Number and Party Entitled to Dispose.
Click "Execute" to display all logged changes for the specified product and timeframe.Benefits of Product Change Logs: 

Enhanced transparency and traceability of changes made to warehouse products.
Improved control over master data modifications.
Robust auditing capabilities for compliance purposes.
This feature is a valuable addition for organizations aiming to maintain accurate records of warehouse operations and ensure accountability within their EWM systems.

 

Reference SAP notes, 3485825 - Activation & display of Warehouse Product change documents

 

Best Regards,

Kannan Chokkanathan.

entry.title: Re: Receiving E-Invoices with SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
The proposed solution here, with the XSLT transformation, is very insufficient. According to GoBD (And other laws in Germany) the original incoming invoice needs to be validated and stored in an audit-proof way. At least the validation step is completely missing in the proposed solution!

How do you know that the e-invoice is valid? Does it implement the correct syntax of CII or UBL? Are all used unit codes from the UN/ECE codelist? Does it contain all mandatory fields? Are the cardinalities correct? And so on  and so on .. depending on the e-invoice there can be multiple hundreds of attributes which need to be validated. And non valid invoices needs to be rejected. Accepting and paying a non valid e-invoice is the same as accepting and paying a regular invoice without invoice-no & tax-no. The tax office always suspects money laundering and VAT fraud. Good luck with that! 

At ZEIT.IO we have a validator and viewer for e-invoices. You can simply upload your e-invoice here and you will get a validation report and a visualisation for the XML: https://zeit.io/de/e-rechnung 
The uploaded invoice on that page is deleted after the analysing step. 

That functionality is available via the ZEIT.IO REST API as well, for registered users. And I would be interested to convert all kind of e-invoices to the native SAP ByD invoice format. I guess that is

n0:FormInvoiceRequest

To implement a converter I would need the complete Schema for the SAP invoice XML. As far as I know the schema is "http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global". But there can I find the full schema definition? I googled for it but I didn't found anything. Can anyone point me to the full schema definition? That would be awesome!

It should be 1620

entry.title: Re: Clean core extensibility: what are "types of scope fo...
entry.content:
Thank you very much!

So, it's how SAP name at a very high level the groups of software functionalities provided by SAP versus needed by a customer. It's a very high-level notion that is not part of software and not needed by developers to practice extensibility.

In the chapter What is a Software Extension. Copyrighted image:

« All software is designed with a scope in mind.

  • Delivered scope: SAP standard software
  • Covered scope: Required by customer, covered by SAP standard
  • Required scope: Required by customer
  • Specific scope: Specific for a customer, industry, or region. Covered by SAP S/4HANA Industry Solutions and localization.
  • Differentiating scope: [part of Specific scope which gives a] competitive advantage for customer. Customer never wants this to be part of SAP standard.
  • Uncovered scope: Required by customer. Not yet covered by SAP standard. »

NB: for my next searches, I guess I should find easily if I'd Google by scoping only the tutorial pages e.g.: https://www.google.com/search?q=software+scope+site%3Alearning.sap.com%2Flearning-journeys%2Fpracticing-clean-core-extensibility-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud.

entry.title: Katowice CodeJam - partner wydarzenia poszukiwany
entry.content:
Cześć SAP Community Katowice,

W 2025 roku mamy ambitne plany organizacji kilku wydarzeń w Katowicach dla członków i członkiń SAP Community Katowice (poza SAP Stammtisch, który się odbywa dość często). Do tego jednak potrzebna jest Wasza pomoc 🙂

Poszukujemy partnera, firmę która udostępni swoją przestrzeń do zorganizowania SAP CodeJam w marcu lub kwietniu. Zapytajcie swoich pracodawców i dajcie znać w komentarzu pod postem lub w wiadomości bezpośredniej: @ewelinas 

Częścią organizacyjną, wraz z prelegentem, który poprowadzi SAP CodeJam, zajmie się SAP Community.

Możliwe tematy SAP CodeJam:

* Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core *

* Service integration with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model *

* Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh *

entry.title: Katowice SAP Meetup - partner i prelegenci wydarzenia poszukiwani
entry.content:
Cześć SAP Community Katowice,

W 2025 roku mamy ambitne plany organizacji kilku wydarzeń w Katowicach dla członków i członkiń SAP Community Katowice (poza SAP Stammtisch, który się odbywa dość często). Do tego jednak potrzebna jest Wasza pomoc 🙂

Poszukujemy partnera, firmę która udostępni swoją przestrzeń do zorganizowania SAP Meetup w kwietniu lub maju. 

Poszukiwani są również 3 prelegenci, którzy mogą poprowadzić SAP Meetup i zaprezentować ciekawy temat SAPowy w ciągu około 30 minut wystąpienia.

Dajcie znać w komentarzu pod postem kto byłby chętny we wsparciu tej inicjatywy lub w wiadomości bezpośredniej: @ewelinas 

SAP Community pomoże w części organizacyjnej i marketingowej wydarzenia.

entry.title: Re: Doubt for optimize abap code
entry.content:
You forgot to format your code, let me do it for you (+ missing arobase added):

 

DATA lt_sobid  TYPE TABLE OF hrp1001-sobid.
DATA lt_posids TYPE TABLE OF hrp1001-objid.

" Collect all position IDs first 
LOOP AT is_ee_org_ass_repl_req-org_assignment INTO DATA(ls_org_assignment).
  APPEND ls_org_assignment-position_id TO lt_posids.
ENDLOOP.

" Ensure the table is not empty to prevent runtime errors 
IF lt_posids IS NOT INITIAL.
  " Fetch all relevant sobids in a single SELECT statement 
  SELECT sobid
    FROM hrp1001 CLIENT SPECIFIED
    INTO TABLE @LT_sobid
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_posids
    WHERE mandt  = @SY-mandt
      AND plvar  = '01'
      AND objid  = @LT_posids
      AND begda <= @LV_fecha_final
      AND endda >= @LV_fecha_final
      AND subty  = 'A012'.
ENDIF.

" Processing the results as needed 
LOOP AT lt_sobid INTO DATA(ls_sobid).
  " Your logic here using ls_sobid 
ENDLOOP.

entry.title: Suggestions on Updating Breakdown Notifications and Equipment Status

Dear Experts,

I am seeking your guidance regarding an issue we are currently facing. Our client requires daily updates of the breakdown status in the equipment user status. While the site team is already opening breakdown notifications, the client has proposed giving all end users access to IE02 for updating the user status daily.

However, granting end users this access could pose a significant risk, as it allows changes to the equipment master data. We currently have around 10 vehicles in breakdown status at a single site, and our organization operates approximately 35 sites.

From my understanding, daily updates to the user status are generally more applicable to production or manufacturing companies.

Could you please suggest the best procedure for updating breakdown notifications or equipment user status? Is providing IE02 access to all end users advisable, or is there an alternative, more secure approach we should consider?

entry.title: Boosting Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud: Simple and Efficient Strategies
entry.content:
Objective: Optimize the performance in SAP Analytics Cloud using simple and efficient steps.

I strongly believe data modeling is best optimized in Datasphere rather than HANA modeling. Therefore, it is possible to enhance the performance of dashboards in SAC by following the steps mentioned below:

  1. Follow widget weightage rules by SAP.
  2. Implementing Apply buttons for input control, measure control, and dimension control.
  3. Filter Optimization: Use Story Filters Instead of Page Filters.
  4. Collapse Page Filters and Input Controls

Let breakdown each thing,

Follow Widget Weightage Rules by SAP

  • Overview: SAP Analytics Cloud assigns widget weightage based on complexity and resource consumption. To ensure optimal performance, developing dashboards while adhering to the widget weightage rule is advisable. If multiple KPIs need to be developed, they should be distributed across separate pages, maintaining a recommended total weight of 5 units per page.
  • Performance Considerations: Developing 14 to 20 KPIs and multiple input controls on a single page may be optimized for design but could impact performance.
  • Best Practice: Instead of placing all KPIs on one page, split them according to the widget weightage rule, ensuring no more than 5 units per page. This will help in achieving an optimized, high-performing dashboard.
  • For better understanding visit: Apply Best Practices for Performance Optimization in Story Design | SAP Help Portal

 

  • Optimization Tips:
    • Use Optimized Design Experiences to create stories.
    • Limit the number of widgets on a single page.
    • Go with optimized charts such as bar or line charts instead of complex visualizations where possible.
    • Minimize the use of widgets with high rendering weights, such as maps or complex hierarchical tables.
    • Leverage the Performance Analyzer to assess whether the story is optimized and identify any issues. It will display which widgets or scripts take longer to load.

Implement Apply Buttons for Input Control, Measure Control, Dimension Control

  • Implementing an Apply Button for input, measure, and dimension controls is an effective way to optimize performance in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) dashboards.
  • By using an Apply button, you allow users to make multiple selections or changes without triggering data recalculations or page refreshes with every single input. Instead, the system will only process the changes when the user explicitly clicks the Apply button, minimizing unnecessary data reloads and improving dashboard performance.

Benefits of Using an Apply Button:

  1. Improved Performance: By delaying the data refresh until the user clicks the Apply button, you reduce the load on the system, especially for complex stories with multiple filters.
  2. User Control: Users can select multiple filters or options at once and see the effect of their selections only after applying them, leading to a more controlled and predictable user experience.
  3. Avoid Redundant Queries: Without an Apply button, the system may trigger data queries for every change made to the input controls, which can slow down performance. The Apply button ensures that only the necessary queries are triggered when needed.
  4. In this case, the dashboard triggers a data query every time the user changes a filter without the apply button and with the apply button. Here's an example of how SQL queries would be triggered

 

ScenarioSQL Queries
Without Apply

Query 1: SELECT * FROM sales_data WHERE Invoice_Date IN ('2020', '2021', '2022')

Query 2: SELECT * FROM sales_data WHERE Invoice_Date IN ('2020', '2021', '2022') AND Region IN ('South', 'Southeast')

 
With ApplyQuery (After Apply): SELECT * FROM sales_data WHERE Invoice_Date IN ('2020', '2021', '2022') AND Region IN ('South', 'Southeast')

Apply Button

Impact on Performance:

  • Without Apply Button: Multiple queries are sent to the data source for each filter change, causing extra load and slowing down the dashboard performance, especially with larger datasets or complex queries.
  • With Apply Button: Only a single query is triggered after the user applies their filter selections, improving performance by reducing the number of queries and data retrieval times.

Filter Optimization: Use Story Filters Instead of Page Filters

 

If the same filter/input control is repeated across all pages in a story, use a Story Filter (Global Filter) instead of Page Filters. Here's why:

  1. Consistency Across Pages:
    A Story Filter applies the same filter to all pages, ensuring uniformity in data across the story.
  2. Simplified User Interaction:
    Users don’t need to set the same filter on every page; changing it once updates all pages.
  3. Reduced Complexity:
    Managing one Story Filter is simpler than maintaining multiple Page Filters with identical logic.
  4. Improved Performance for Shared Filters:
    Since the filter is applied globally, it reduces redundancy in querying and ensures a smoother user experience.

Usage:
Apply a Story Filter when the same filter (e.g., Region or Date) is required across all pages to maintain a consistent context and improve usability.

Collapse Page Filters and Input Controls

Expanded page filters or input controls are convenient because you can quickly select members or search for members in the control. However, an expanded input control refreshes often and that can affect the story's performance.

When the input control is collapsed, you must select the control to display the member list before you can search or make your selections.

 
 

Hope this helps you guys.

Thank you.

                        </PricingElement>
                </BillableDocumentItem>
            </BillableDocument>
        </sd:BillingDocumentRequest>
    </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

 

Questions:

  1. What specific pricing issues could cause this error, even though the document is saved?
  2. How can I identify the exact pricing condition causing the error?
  3. Are there any common configuration gaps (e.g., access sequences, missing condition records) that I should recheck?
  4. Does anyone have tips for debugging this error further using AIF or any other tools in SAP Public Cloud?

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Downtime-optimized Upgrades and Hotfix Collection Imports
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk") provides an "always up-to-date" environment. Currently, these updates do require planned technical downtimes. The downtimes are covered by contractual SLAs and the schedule is announced in advanced for the whole calendar year, for example, for 2025 in this blog post Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows. 

Our goal is to reduce the maintenance windows significantly. According to our roadmap, we are providing the first step of Downtime-optimized Upgrades and Hotfix Collection Imports with the upgrade to release 2502.

Although the contractual SLA remains unchanged for this first step, you will benefit from a reduced technical downtime of upgrades and patches in case of non-development systems (test and productive systems).

This blog post gives you more insights about the scope and the characteristics of the new approach.

 

How Does It Work?

The new downtime-optimized upgrade procedure splits the upgrade process into three phases:

  1. Preparation
  2. Takeover
  3. Post processing

During the preparation phase, the new release of the software stack gets installed into a shadow layer of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment system. This installation happens in the background, while the system is up and running. The new software release is not visible to the running applications yet, and business users can keep using the system as usual. However, certain lifecycle activities would interfere with the installation process and will be blocked while the background installation is running. This includes the import of new transports, the lifecycle of customer-managed software, and the lifecycle of tenants.

Once the background installation has finished, the system is ready for the takeover phase. In this phase, the system switches to the new software release. The takeover induces a system downtime in the area of currently 20 to 80 minutes. For upgrades, the required downtime depends mostly on the amount of the custom development. Running ABAP applications will be shut down, including active ABAP sessions. After the takeover, the system will come back online and all applications will be running on the new software release. Business users can start working on the system again.

The finalization of the upgrade and required clean up steps happen in the post processing phase. These steps will run in background, while the system is fully available for business users. Afterwards, all transport and lifecycle event restrictions are lifted.

For hotfix collection imports, the process is quite similar with the difference that the takeover duration is much shorter: About 10 minutes in 2502. A reduction to 2 minutes is expected in later releases.

 

Scope

The SAP BTP ABAP environment systems are automatically selected for the downtime-optimized release upgrade and hotfix import procedures based on certain system criteria. There is no manual nomination possible and also no service request required like a customer ticket. Two criteria need to be met that a system is chosen for the downtime-optimized release upgrade and hotfix import procedures:

  • The system is configured as a non-development system.
  • The system has at least 4 HANA Compute Units (HCUs).

 

First Usage in Release 2502

Upgrade

The upgrade schedule (see figure Upgrade 2502) will remain the same as with the standard upgrade procedure that is still used for development systems: The upgrade starts up to 5 hours, latest 2 hours before the given maintenance window. With the new downtime-optimized procedure the majority of the upgrade work is done in background during system uptime before the maintenance window begins. This is an improvement compared to the standard procedure, where the majority of the upgrade work is done within the maintenance window. In this first rollout phase the system downtime is not guaranteed to match the start time of the announced maintenance window and might happen later. The reason is, that the lead time of 2 to 5 hours across all systems is not yet aligned with the runtimes needed to reach the announced maintenance window. The announced maintenance windows can be found here.

Figure: Upgrade 2502

 

Hotfix Collection Imports

The update schedule (see figure Update 2502) for the downtime-optimized update differs from the standard update procedure that is still used for development systems. The standard procedure update starts in background up to 120 minutes, latest 40 minutes before the given maintenance window. The downtime-optimized update procedure is started several hours earlier to account for the longer total runtime. Similar to the new upgrade procedure, the new downtime-optimized procedure does the majority of the update work during system uptime and before the maintenance window starts. This is an improvement compared to the standard procedure, where the majority of the update work is done within the maintenance window. The exact takeover time is not guaranteed to match the announced maintenance window start time and might begin later depending on the system individual runtime.

 Figure: Update 2502

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay-Payout curves-Interpolation

@rkumar1947 Yes, Flexible Payout Curves does support Interpolation. I haven't had time to document it but it's not really complicated, you just take the points from business goal Min/Low/Default/High/Max and add them to separate lines in the Flexible Payout Curve file. So for example for scenario 4:

You can add an unlimited number of lines/points for each goals (vs 5 in Admin Center UI). But at the end of the day it doesn't change anything to the logic applied to the calculation which the one explained in the blog.

All the best

entry.title: Using Table Lookup substitution type in Manage Substitution/Validation application in UPA system
entry.content:
Hi Everyone

In this post we will see how to use Table Lookup in Manage Substitution rule for substitute value in field using CBO (Custom Business Object.

In Universal Parallel Accounting 2023 (3191636), there are many functions are enhanced and made Business process easy with new Fiori Application. New changes incorporated which include addition of new functionality such as Ledger wise settlement, Event based revenue recognition etc. and certain things been obsolete. In UPA 2023 

  • Evaluation group fields are obsolete; use extensibility tools instead. See also the entries “Evaluation groups” and “Extensibility: Custom fields for asset master data” above.
  • Group assets are obsolete.

well if the requirement is to club Assets of certain Asset class into one group and so on respectively. We will use CFL (Custom Field Logic) Fiori App for which you can follow my previous blog , Custom Business Object and Manage Substitution/validation for Asset master to achieve grouping of Assets of certain asset class.

Step1: Creation of Custom Field using Custom Field and Logic Fiori App - In order to fill the field with value automatically when master is created in company code and asset master, we need field to be created for asset master. Standard field can also be leverage to substitute with value. Please follow my previous blog for creating custom field in master date using Custom Field and Logic fiori application.

Step2: Creating Custom Business Object - Custom Business Objects (CBO) is a tool used to create own business objects for use in database tables or as the basis for custom applications. For simple understanding consider CBO as object which have database table in backend which means CBO can be referred and used as table. To create CBO login to Fiori application "Custom Business Objects"

Name CBO, kindly note each CBO start with ZZ1 concatenate name you provide as  identifier

Click on "Create" and post use "Fields" tab of Edit CBO in order to create fields require as per business requirement. In our requirement we will add to two fields in CBO, one for storing Asset class and other to store Group number an Asset class will fall under respectively. Finally click on "Publish" to have CBO activate and ready for use. Please take help of technical team member in order to fill the data in CBO or else its respective backend table can be view in content of data definition marked in yellow color

Once backend data table is identified, we will add the Asset classes and respective group number we want to substitute. Below is table filled with both Asset class such as 1100, 2000 and group number 123, 678 respectively.

Step3 - Creating Substitution Rule using Fiori application "Manage substitution and validation rules - for asset master data" App ID:F6968.

Substitution rule will be create which will take reference from CBO using substitution type Table Lookup to substitute data automatically with Asset group when asset is created in Asset class mapped in backend table as shown above. In our example no precondition is filled as we want this rule to be applicable on all the company code across the client. But if you have requirement to make this applicable to certain Company code or other attribute then do select in precondition

things to note here is that "Target Field" in Substitution is the field in Asset Master data where value will get substitute and Source is the field in CBO i.e. Asset group number in our example. So whenever Asset master will be created in asset class respective Asset group number from CBO will filled in Asset master custom field created to store the group number

Our custom field created to store asset group number in asset master data get substitute automatically with CBO field value of respective asset class on the creation of asset master data.

Hope this blog is helpful.

Let me know if you have any question or provide feedback if any

thanks

Raunak Bhatia

entry.title: Re: How to Exclude English Business Words from Auto-Trans...
entry.content:
Hi Justin,

SAP Enable Now uses SAP Translation Hub to translate manually entered text.  There is no way to modify the modify how SAP Translation Hub translates content from within Enable Now.  so, if there is any way to do this, the modification must be done on the Translation Hub side but when I have previously asked the Translation Hub team about this, I was informed that this was not possible.

I would repost this question on an SAP Translation Hub community, if one exists, and if not, on a BTP community.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: User role cannot access workarea
entry.content:
Hi there,

There are two types of permissions in Enable Now, User Permissions that are added to User Roles to allow users to access Enable Now functionality.  I think this is the functionality that you are using.

The second type of permission in Enable Now is a Workarea Permission. Workarea Permissions control access to the content in a Workarea.  To modify these:

1. Open Manager
2. Select Administration > Workspaces and Tags
3. Click the Manage Access link beside the Workspace that you are trying to control
4. Add a User Role, e.g. Administrators, to the Selection field and then give that User Role the correct access to the Workspace
5. Click OK after adding all the relevant User Roles and specifying the level of access for each Role

Administrators should have full access to the Workspace.  Master and Standard Authors should be able to create, edit and delete content, and Learners should only be able to view published content.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Staging with serial numbers via MIGO
entry.content:
We are staging for service orders and do this with transaction MIGO posting the goods issue. This leads to the outbound delivery in S4 and in Embedded EWM. We are also handling material which is relevant for serial numbers. Our goal is to avoid to enter the serial number in MIGO but at the process of picking in EWM. The worker shall decide which serial number has to be taken. 

As our serial number profile demands a mandatory serial number at the delivery and the goods movement, we have to enter the serial numbers already in MIGO. Does someone know a possibility to avoid this in SAP-standard? Was hoping that there is a standard solution in EWM. Didn‘t find a fine solution for that besides exits or enhancements.

entry.title: Re: Authors failing to create documentation in producer
entry.content:
Hi Mira,

There are multiple reasons that Enable Now stops generating documentation, e.g. companies upgrade their Microsoft Office suite to an unsupported version of Office 365.  There are a few options to progress this issue:

1. Try opening and clearing the Javascript Console in Producer - select View > Javascript Console - and then generate the documentation again.  Check the Console for an error, this may help you to identify the problem
2. Use this KBA to troubleshoot your issue.  You will need to download and run an executable file, so you might have to get your IT team involved, but that executable may identify the issue with your systems
3. Raise an SAP support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and get support involved

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP BTP and On-Premise SAP Business One using the SAP Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hello @kruzikruz 

"Option 2: Expose the Service Layer through the SAP Cloud Connector" is only for the BTP development. then you set up the BTP destination like the above screenshot and use the destination as a service connection when you create a new project in Build Code, Build Apps and etc.

If it's a 3rd party app like the react native application. the above option doesn't work. you need to go with the "Option 1: Expose the Service Layer over the internet using a reverse proxy". in this case, you need to modernize the SAP Business One Landscape using reverse proxy: Best Practices for Technical Implementation of SAP Business One Web Client . then service layer can be accessible just like you use the cloud odata endpoint.

 

Regards
EunSeok

entry.title: Integrated FI-GR not releasing data into Group Reporting when Journal Release is executed.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a confusing problem with FI-GR integration.
We have set up preparation ledger(0L), assigned same FYV in GR as we have in FI and configured the version. We have mapped all relevant  FSI and CUs as well. However, when I try to run Journal Release, it only gave warning and no data is released. Here are the observed sys behaviours:

  1. When Journal Release is executed for CUs with existing FI data, no data is released and only a warning is given as shown below.
  2. When FI data is uploaded into a company code via DMC, Journal release for the corresponding CU in GR does not import any data as well. It only gives same warning message.
  3. When fresh journal entries are made in FI, journal release for the corresponding CU run successfully. 
  4. I also observed that the GR fields are not shown when I view posted journals via Manage Journal Entries app:

At this point, I am completely lost on what else to do. I appreciate all the help I can get.

Thanks & Regards,
Seyi.

entry.title: Re: Integrated FI-GR not releasing data into Group Report...
entry.content:
Data Release behavior differs based on whether the GRPL is activated for the posting period of the journals uploaded in ACDOCA.
If GRPL is activated for 2024 and onwards, and you upload journals with posting fiscal year = 2024, then you need to check whether GRPL derivation works for those journals, especially FSI and CU. If no, you need to verify your FSI / GL mapping excel and CU definition or GRPL substitution rules which overwrite the default derivation logic. Then run the realignment job to make sure that GR derivation reflected with those journals and try data release via data monitor which is on delta mode by default.
